trade.summary: Italia is a significant EU trading partner for Israele. Israelei exports include machinery, pharmaceuticals, and hi-tech equipment; Italian exports include machinery, chemicals, and fashion goods. Trade has been broadly stable though impacted by regional conflict in 2024-2025.

diplomatic.summary: Italia maintains full diplomatic relations with Israele and has historically been a moderating voice within the EU. Italia expressed solidarity after the October 2023 Hamas attack but also called for humanitarian corridors in Gaza. Relations have been somewhat strained by the prolonged Gaza conflict and Italian domestic political pressures.

military.summary: Both countries are NATO-aligned though Israele is not a member. Italia and Israele cooperate on defense technology and intelligence sharing. Italia participated in Red Sea escort missions that intersected with Israelei security interests. No formal bilateral defense treaty.

financial.summary: Moderate bilateral investment flows. Italian companies have invested in Israelei tech sector; Israelei firms have some presence in Italian market. No major financial tensions.

energy.summary: Italia has shown interest in Israelei and Eastern Mediterranean gas as a diversification from Russian supplies. ENI has participated in regional gas discussions. Limited direct bilateral energy trade to date.

scientific.summary: Strong academic and scientific ties. Italia and Israele have bilateral S&T cooperation agreements. Joint publications in life sciences, physics, and materials science are frequent. Several joint university programs exist.
